learningObjectives: Name and use twelve culture and festival words at Flyers A2 level.
Use irregular past verbs went, saw and had to talk about festivals.

grammarReference.structure: Past simple: irregular verbs (went, saw, had)
grammarReference.explanation: Use went (not goed) for movement in the past: We went to the parade. Use saw (not seed) for seeing: We saw fireworks. Use had (not haved) for experiences or meals: We had a big party. These verbs do not add -ed.
examples: Last Tet we went to my grandparents' house.
Minh saw a colourful parade in the city.
We had lucky money and a family dinner.
Linh went to school in her ao dai costume.
commonMistakes: We goed to the parade (×) → We went to the parade (✓)
I seed the fireworks (×) → I saw the fireworks (✓)
They haved a party (×) → They had a party (✓)

grammarReference.explanation: Use When + past simple to say what happened at one moment in a story. When we arrived, we saw decorations. When the parade started, everyone cheered. The when-clause often comes first, then a comma.
examples: When we arrived, Grandma had food ready.
When the music started, Linh began to dance.
When we saw the fireworks, we clapped loudly.
When Minh went to the festival, he wore a costume.
commonMistakes: When we arrive, we saw food (×) → When we arrived, we saw food (✓)
When we arrived we saw food (×) → When we arrived, we saw food (✓) — comma after clause
When we went, we go to the parade (×) → When we went, we saw the parade (✓)

grammarReference.explanation: Combine irregular past verbs with when-clauses to tell festival stories in order. First say when something happened, then what you went to, saw or had. Keep both verbs in the past.
examples: When we arrived at school, we saw costumes and music.
We went to the national parade and had a wonderful holiday.
When Linh saw the fireworks, she had a big smile.
Minh went home after the party and had a rest.
commonMistakes: When we arrived, we see fireworks (×) → When we arrived, we saw fireworks (✓)
We went and have a party (×) → We went and had a party (✓)
When they saw, they goed home (×) → When they saw, they went home (✓)

lessons.title: Lesson 1: Understand Chronological Links
lessons.learningObjective: Follow chronological links (first, when, then, after) in a festival text.

exercises.title: Learn: Order of Events
exercises.instructions: Read the passage. Answer the questions about order.

passage.title: Minh's Tet Holiday
passage.text:
Minh is eleven and lives in Hanoi. Last Tet was a special national holiday for his family. First, Minh and his sister Linh helped Mum clean the house. It is an old custom before every festival.

When they arrived at their grandparents' house, they saw red decorations and lucky money on the table. Grandma had prepared a big family party with traditional food. Minh wore a colourful ao dai costume — an important tradition in Vietnam.

In the afternoon the family went to the city centre. They watched a long parade with music and dance groups. When the parade finished, everyone went to Hoan Kiem Lake. They saw bright fireworks in the night sky and clapped loudly.

At the end of the holiday Minh felt happy. He wrote about the festival for his school project and told his class why customs and traditions matter.

questions.questionText: What did Minh and Linh do FIRST before going to grandparents'?
questions.explanation: First they helped Mum clean the house — câu đầu có First.

questions.questionText: When they arrived at grandparents' house, what did they see?
questions.explanation: Red decorations and lucky money — mệnh đề When we arrived.

When they arrived at their grandparents' house, they saw red decorations and lucky money on the table. Grandma had prepared a big family party with traditional food. Minh wore a colourful ao dai costume — an important tradition in Vietnam.

In the afternoon the family went to the city centre. They watched a long parade with music and dance groups. When the parade finished, everyone went to Hoan Kiem Lake. They saw bright fireworks in the night sky and clapped loudly.

At the end of the holiday Minh felt happy. He wrote about the festival for his school project and told his class why customs and traditions matter.

script.title: Planning the School Culture Festival
script.setting: School classroom, culture club meeting after lessons

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Welcome! We must plan our school culture festival for next month.

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: When we had the festival last year, I saw a wonderful dance on stage.

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: Can we celebrate national traditions with costumes and music?

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Yes. Each class can wear a traditional costume for the parade.

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: We went to the gym last time. Should we go there again?

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Good idea. When we arrive, we will set up the stage first.

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: Can we have fireworks? Well, maybe just colourful lights instead.

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Right. Let's also invite parents for a small party after the show.

script.audioNotes: Teacher-led school festival planning, three speakers, moderate pace. Approx. 55 seconds.

script.title: Watching the National Parade
script.setting: City street near Hoan Kiem Lake, national holiday morning

lines.speaker: Dad
lines.text: Look — the national parade is starting now!

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: When we arrived, we saw dancers in bright costumes.

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: I love the music! Last year we had seats near the front.

lines.speaker: Dad
lines.text: We went early today, so we can see everything clearly.

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: When the band passed, I saw our school flag in the parade.

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: After the parade we can celebrate with ice cream.

lines.speaker: Dad
lines.text: Tonight we will watch fireworks by the lake. It is a Tet custom.

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: This holiday is my favourite. Festivals bring the whole city together.

script.audioNotes: Family watching national parade dialogue, warm tone. Approx. 50 seconds.
